note: Glosses are editorial (English authoritative, 中文 careful); manuscript & listing counts are read from the catalogue.

glosses.en: Jatukham Ramathep — a guardian-deity amulet cult that swept Thailand in the 2000s.
glosses.zh: 加都堪拉瑪贴 — 二〇〇〇年代風靡泰國的護法神護符。

glosses.en: A spell inscribed on a thin metal scroll, rolled tight and empowered — worn for protection, invulnerability, or love.
glosses.zh: 符管 — 刻於薄金屬並捲緊加持的經文護符，用於護身、刀槍不入或招情。

entries.term: เครื่องราง

glosses.en: Talisman or charm, in the broad sense — any empowered object worn or carried for its power.
glosses.zh: 護身符（廣義）— 一切經加持、隨身佩帶以求靈力的物件。

entries.term: พระเครื่อง

glosses.en: A Buddhist amulet, usually a small moulded image of a Buddha or revered monk.
glosses.zh: 佛牌 — 多為佛陀或高僧的小型模製聖像護符。

glosses.en: The Somdej amulet — among the most revered Thai amulet types, the 'king of amulets.'
glosses.zh: 崇迪佛牌 — 最受尊崇的泰國佛牌之一，有『佛牌之王』之稱。

glosses.en: Auspicious sacred objects — the umbrella category for amulets, charms and consecrated items.
glosses.zh: 聖物 · 吉祥物 — 佛牌、護符與開光物件的總稱。

glosses.en: The eclipse-deity — the shadow-planet that swallows sun and moon; propitiated to turn misfortune to fortune.
glosses.zh: 羅睺 — 食日月的影曜之神，供奉以轉厄為福。

entries.term: โหราศาสตร์

glosses.en: Astrology — the reckoning of time, star and fate; the diviner's science of auspicious days and birth-charts.
glosses.zh: 占星術 — 推算時辰、星曜與命運，擇吉日、排命盤之學。

glosses.en: Loving-kindness — as a magical quality, the power to draw goodwill and warmth from all who meet you.
glosses.zh: 慈心 — 作為法門的特質，能招來眾人的善意與愛戴。

entries.term: เมตตามหานิยม

glosses.en: Loving-kindness + 'great popularity' — charm that makes one widely liked and favoured.
glosses.zh: 慈心廣受愛戴 — 使人緣廣結、備受喜愛的法門。

entries.term: สีผึ้งเสน่ห์

glosses.en: Enchanted lip-wax — a balm consecrated to lend the wearer allure.
glosses.zh: 招情蜂蠟 — 開光的唇蠟，增添佩者魅力。

glosses.en: Love-drawing allure — charms and rites to kindle desire and attraction.
glosses.zh: 魅惑 · 招情 — 引動愛慕與情緣的法術。

entries.term: ท้าวเวสสุวรรณ

glosses.en: Vaishravana — guardian king of the north, giant lord of yakṣas, who wards off ghosts and evil.
glosses.zh: 多聞天王（毗沙門）— 北方護法天王，夜叉之主，驅鬼辟邪。

glosses.en: A Pali incantation — verses of power, recited to activate a charm, ward danger, or draw fortune.
glosses.zh: 咒語 — 巴利語的靈驗偈頌，誦唸以啟動法物、驅邪或招福。

glosses.en: The sacred syllable นะ — a seed-letter at the heart of countless katha and yantra; the tradition counts 108 of its forms.
glosses.zh: 「那」聖音字 — 眾多咒與符的種子字，傳有一百零八變化。

entries.term: กะลาตาเดียว

glosses.en: A rare one-eyed coconut shell — a single natural sprout-hole makes it a powerful protective charm.
glosses.zh: 獨眼椰殼 — 天然僅一芽孔的稀有椰殼，為強力護符。

glosses.en: A phallic amulet — carved for protection, virility and warding of harm.
glosses.zh: 陽形護符 — 雕製以護身、旺陽、避害。

entries.term: เขี้ยวเสือ

glosses.en: Tiger fang — worn for authority, command over others and protection.
glosses.zh: 虎牙 — 佩以增威權、懾眾、護身。

entries.term: พญาเต่าเรือน

glosses.en: The turtle amulet — for longevity and steady, sheltering wealth.
glosses.zh: 龜將軍 — 象徵長壽與穩固招財。

glosses.en: A cowrie-shell remedy charm — the classic guard against black magic and curse.
glosses.zh: 解厄寶螺 — 對治邪術與詛咒的傳統寶貝護符。

glosses.en: Medicine — herbal remedy and the medical manuals; healing sits inside wichaa, not apart from it.
glosses.zh: 藥 · 草藥 — 醫方與醫書；療癒本屬 wichaa 之內，不與其分。

glosses.en: Invulnerability — the classic martial protection, skin that blades cannot cut.
glosses.zh: 刀槍不入 — 經典的護體法門，刀刃不能傷。

glosses.en: Chewed betel-quid — spat and worked into folk blessing, healing and love magic.
glosses.zh: 檳榔渣 — 用於民間祝福、療癒與情術。

glosses.en: The ritual master's knife — for exorcism, cutting malign influence, and consecrating charms.
glosses.zh: 法師刀 — 用於驅邪、斬煞與開光法物。

entries.term: น้ำมันมนต์

glosses.en: Enchanted oil — consecrated for love, luck or persuasion, anointed on skin or object.
glosses.zh: 法油 · 咒油 — 為招情、招運或說服而開光，塗於身或物。

glosses.en: Sacred powder of Buddha-virtue — ground from consecrated materials and pressed into amulets.
glosses.zh: 佛粉（聖粉）— 由開光材料研磨，壓入佛牌之中。

glosses.en: The 'golden boy' — a child-spirit effigy kept and fed for luck, wealth and protection.
glosses.zh: 金童 — 供養以求財、護佑的靈童造像。

glosses.en: The beckoning lady — her raised hand calls in customers and wealth; the merchant's charm.
glosses.zh: 招財女神 — 舉手招客招財，商家的守護。

glosses.en: Ai Khai — the boy-spirit of Wat Chedi, famed for granting luck and answered wishes.
glosses.zh: 艾蓋童子 — 柴迪寺的靈童，以賜運、有求必應聞名。

entries.term: หลวงปู่ทวด

glosses.en: A revered southern monk (16th–17th c.), whose amulets are believed to protect from harm and misfortune.
glosses.zh: 龍菩托祖師 — 受尊崇的南方高僧，其佛牌被信能護身避險。

glosses.en: The ascetic hermit-seers — the teacher-figures at the root of the tradition, patrons of yantra, katha and healing.
glosses.zh: 修行隱士 · 仙人 — 傳統根源的祖師，符、咒與醫術的守護者。

glosses.en: A yantra inked onto cloth — a protective banner or wearing-cloth carrying the diagram.
glosses.zh: 符布 — 繪有法陣的護身布或旗。

glosses.en: A sacred diagram of letters, syllables and lines — drawn, inscribed or tattooed to protect, empower, or attract. The north's core visual magic.
glosses.zh: 符 · 護身法陣 — 由聖字、音節與線條構成的幾何圖，用以護身、加持或招引。
